item.title: Re: Image credit
item.content:encoded:
K.

Edit: To be clear, the image is now removed and the file has been deleted from the page.

item.title: Image credit
item.content:encoded:
as pointed out by RequisiteFahrenheit in 2012, the image is taken from the trailer to the 1975 Woody Allen film Love and Death.

Movie trailers are only public domain if they were published prior to 1977 without a copyright notice; I've just watched the trailer on Youtube and it does have a copyright notice, at the very end. Therefore, the image is not public domain and is not compatible with the SCP Foundation wiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Perhaps.

Such is the way of mysterious mysteries.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this, kinda mysterious. I still have to read the 2012 incident but are you gonna make a 2018 one this november?

item.title: Update 4/22/17
item.content:encoded:
Alright, so a major critique of this article when it was first posted was the lack of GRU-P involvement in an anomalous object located in Cold War Belarus, and how the it was unlikely the Foundation would have been able to access a site to contain an anomaly behind the Iron Curtain. I've gone ahead and updated the article to address this concern.

Now, the Foundation only contained the object after 1992, with all other information pieced together from GRU-P records.

For those of you who downvoted previously because of this issue, I'd say it might be worth your time to give this another look. Or not. I cannot tell you what to do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Always happy to help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for getting me interested enough to read a series of tales.

item.title: Re: Just so you guys know.
item.content:encoded:
It's more like a hypocorism, not a proper name. While it could be used as an official name, it's weird and uncommon. As you go back in time, it becomes less weird, but Pyotr would still be at least just as valid. And even as a hypocorism, Piatro haven't been used much for decades.

Also, that person that you've linked, if you go to his page on the Belarussian wiki, it lists his name as Пётр.

item.title: Re: Just so you guys know.
item.content:encoded: You are mistaken. Piatro (Пятро) is Belorussian name - for example. Pyotr (Пётр) is Russian version.

item.title: Just so you guys know.
item.content:encoded:
So, anqxyr just pointed out to me that Piatro would in fact not be a Belorussian name. As such, I have changed it to Pyotr.

I am honestly really embarrassed this has been up for so long with such a mistake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It got me a blood +upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it well enough. The coffin full of teeth made me upvote. The idea of reanimation from the scientist experimenting with the occult and chemicals going horribly horribly wrong, but not in a Keter way, just a "wtf happened" sort of way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How exactly is Able going to deal with this? o_0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is. This is odd. o_o I have no idea what I'm feeling towards this one, only that I want to see Able sent to deal with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That last detail was perfect. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I couldn't stop thinking about Super Ghouls and Ghosts when I read this.

It's missing the hook, and seems to arbitrary.

Too many things many changing collections of thematically linked objects for no particular reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love weird ass stuff like this, feels like something I would write. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, though I agree that it could use a stronger hook. +1, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote for now. It's fairly well written, but as others have pointed out, it doesn't have much of a hook. I don't really "get" it, if there's anything to truly get. I can't tell if there's an overall idea behind this that I'm just not picking up on, or if you're just winging it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, an SCP with my birthday! And it's only one year older than me! Interesting idea (Not the date part). It's sort of a creepy version of Brigadoon, with coffins filled with dead people and exploding pigs instead of a charming village filled with Cyd Charisse.

As for whether the Foundation would have been in the USSR during the Soviet era, of course they were. They infiltrated whatever group the soviets set up, ultimately gaining control. At least, that's my headcanon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Chemical induced" sounds clumsy - better to say "chemically induced".

Also, I'm really not keen on the contents log or the pig incident. They seem almost comical. The problem is the reanimation. The headless deer carcass moving about just makes me think of a million kids' ghost stories. The moving human remains - that's just a textbook zombie, isn't it? The hands - now we're getting silly. And as for the pig grenade…

Much creepier if the above were all just plain dead. Then we could have (for instance) a dead pig with a human skeleton. That makes you wonder a lot more about what Piatro ??? was up to.

On that note, while experiments on preservation and reanimation might hint at a reason for the corpses, it doesn't suggest any reason why they're all in the same type of coffin. What's more, why do they all bear a prominent cross if these were purely scientific researches (especially if this was under the purview of the Soviet government, which was officially atheist, and restricted Orthodox worship)? A further hint or two on Piatro ??? and his work is perhaps needed to explain this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The identity of the research aides were expunged with the remainder of the records, so I doubt that one would be readily available for an interview. I'm going to go with the anecdotal evidence route.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like that additional information.

However, if research reports survived, you should write one and put it in a new addendum. Another option would be an interview with a former research aide.

You could also say "Anecdotal evidence suggests that Piatro's research involved …" if you'd prefer not to add a whole new section.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If that's the case, then try this on for size:

"Investigation into the identity of the Piatro █████████ has revealed that the subject was a Minsk mortician and scientist involved in several Soviet projects in the 1950’s. Records indicate the subject passed away on November 15th, 1959. A majority of the records of █████████’s research were destroyed by the Belarusian government prior to 1960. Those that have survived indicate experimentation with long term chemical preservation of human remains, as well as chemical induced reanimation and regeneration. The identities of any other scientists involved on the projects are also unknown as such names were expunged from existing records. Exhumation of Piatro █████████ by research staff has confirmed no anomalous properties with either the remains or burial site."

Is this what you are referring to?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted. Creepy, mysterious and the article was written very well. As long as my opinions are concerned, a great SCP.

Good job, JC!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fuuuuuuuu-
Thank you for pointing that out. Four square kilometers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty neat. I upvoted, but I wanted to point this out:

The surrounding four kilometers are to be enclosed within a chain-link fence topped with barbed wire.

Four square kilometers? Four kilometer radius? Needs clarification.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the obvious route is to expand on Piatro's research. (Maybe the records weren't all destroyed, or we found a clue somewhere.)

Give us a clue as to why the coffins exist—what the original intent was. And remember that the intent can differ from the result.

That said, be careful not to overdivulge because a lot of the creepiness comes from the weirdness and inexplicability. A little extra information can go a long way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Going off of this idea, I have looked over the article again and have thought of the following area that could be expanded upon to provide the hook that is apparently lacking. Let me know what you guys think would be the most appropriate route or if these ideas fall into "Things what are lame" territory.

  • Expand on Piatro Blackbox's research: Since technically his research was destroyed, all his assistants and coworkers could possibly still be alive and responsible for the SCP.
  • Expand on the human remans found in the SCP: The duration that human remains last outside of the caskets could be increasing with each manifestation, with remains becoming fresher and more lifelike with each year, a possible mass animation point could be possible in the near future.
  • Expand on Casket 1760-16: Notes could be found in this casket as the contents become more dangerous, threatening the Foundation to allow public access to the site or face further consequences.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did anyone else think of Persona 3 when they saw the image?

Anyway. I like this. It's creepy and weird and creepyweird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was sort of half-expecting an AWCY thing, but I'm ever so slightly pleasantly surprised that it isn't.

I do have to partially agree with the others though, that there isn't much of a hook. I feel like there IS a hook in there somewhere, but it's too hidden. Holding my vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please move the image you used from the fuel page it's on to this one, otherwise it won't be indexed properly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is just my headcanon speaking, but I don't know if the Foundation would have been operating in the USSR during the Cold War. Seems to me the Soviets would have had their own organization much like Iran has the AGIR.

That's what GRU Division P does.. (and maybe a section of of KGB, too.)

Anyways, I agree with this assessment. This does lack a hook, and to be honest, this as a result of soviet government experimentation, unless we're told about it seems slightly OOC

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is well written and an interesting idea. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote. As others have said, it lacks a real hook, but this is on the right track.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is this really Euclid class? The only danger it's posed has been the result of being meddled with. It's fine if it's kept fenced off, so wouldn't it be Safe class?

It's cool, but it doesn't hook me yet. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1'd for potential.

Other folks have said this needs a hook, and I agree. You've got a lot going on here—maybe pick an element to expand on a bit such that we get a more complete narrative. That'd be one way to get a hook.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Its not that I didn't like your feedback, its that adding a humanoid to this SCP would have made the idea fairly more difficult. I will also say that the suggestion you made was not the direction I intended to take the article.

Also, when no one else posted in that thread beside you it became pretty apparent that thread was a dead end overall and thus I refocused my efforts on feedback via the IRC.

Mmmmm'yep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted at idea, theme and execution. Coffins emerging/submerging with varying contents gives me dark fantasy feeling.

…but you really didn't like my feedback on the draft that you didn't even reply? :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The article is fine but I'm not quite sure about the SCP. Yeah, coffins that surface is a cool concept, but i don't think the contents are that interesting. No vote for now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have cut out "been a variety." The sentence now reads: The past contents of this instance have ranged from packed earth to animated remains. Thank you for pointing that out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep. Twice, just to be sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You don't give me enough credit guys. XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The past contents of this instance have been a variety ranging from packed earth to animated remains

"… have been a variety" doesn't make sense. Might want to consider rewording that sentence.

Also, an exploding pig? Thats comes across as a bit weird for the sake of weird for me to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Edit: Reply moved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: [see link above]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
did anyone else compulsively count the coffins to make sure there were fifteen? =D
hmm, there's an idea for a cognito-hazzard…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: [double post deleted]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh is that where that is from?
I just found that on an scp-fuel thread. XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know what to think of this… it's interesting, but there doesn't seem to be a hook.

This is just my headcanon speaking, but I don't know if the Foundation would have been operating in the USSR during the Cold War. Seems to me the Soviets would have had their own organization much like Iran has the AGIR. (Maybe that's fodder for a tale one of these days.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to say, I love the picture taken from the dream sequence in Woody Allen's 'Love and Death' featuring the dancing waiters emerging from their coffins:

http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pbk9YFxRDqE#t=28s

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't have any strong feelings on this one way or the other. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So… It's a few coffins that rise from the ground once a year. Every six years one of them is special in that it contains corpses of animals which reanimate. Then one time… BAM! Exploding pig!

Going to not vote on this. Maybe in the morning I'll retread it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Once again thank you everyone in the IRC that provided feedback for this article.

JC
